_fixture_meta.describes: A runtime 401 used to distinguish 'exists but auth needed' from 'does not exist'. The error.message confirms the resource exists; this is a verbatim existence-leak in the auth challenge. PRD-109-R3 forbids this — when an unauthenticated request is made for a resource the requester has no permission to know about, the server MUST return 404 (not 401).
_fixture_meta.rejection_reason: 401 reveals the resource exists. The correct response would be 404 with a generic body identical to the 'truly absent' case (see fixtures/109/positive/runtime-404-no-leak.json).

expected_error.missing: 401 used for an existence-confirmation case; expected 404 when the requester is unauthenticated and the scope is private.

error.message: Authentication required to access /act/n/secret-internal-roadmap.json (this resource exists but you are not signed in).
